title: Persona personalization has two tracks: Look and Does
body: I landed on a two-track strategy for persona personalization on index.html. Track 1 (Look) is visual touches — the site swaps taglines, reorders cards, threads the persona accent color through. Lightweight JS reads localStorage, adjusts the page posture. Track 2 (Does) is where the real value lives — each persona unlocks something functionally different. Recruiters get calendar booking and resume download. Founders get a bring your problem mode. Practitioners get teardown director commentary. Learners get ADPList mentorship. Technologists get architecture decision records and GitHub access. Inner Circle gets Flame On by default and a WhatsApp link. The key insight: visual personalization is table stakes. Functional exclusivity is what makes someone feel like their persona choice actually mattered.
decision: Persona personalization operates in two tracks: Track 1 (Look) for visual touches (~14-16 hrs), Track 2 (Does) for persona-exclusive functional unlocks (~50-62 hrs).
alternatives_considered: Could have gone full content reordering per persona (six parallel page experiences), but the complexity does not justify the marginal gain.
open_questions: Will Track 1 visual touches be enough to make the reshaping feel real before Track 2 is built?
